ChatGPT (gpt-4o).subject: Physics
ChatGPT (gpt-4o).question:
In a Young's Double Slit Experiment (YDSE), the slits are separated by 0.45 mm and the screen is placed 2.4 m away.
The distance between the central bright fringe and the fourth bright fringe is measured to be 1.2 cm.
If the entire experimental setup is immersed in a transparent liquid of refractive index μ=1.7, what will be the new fringe width?
ChatGPT (gpt-4o).response: [ERROR: Error code: 429 - {'error': {'message': 'You exceeded your current quota, please check your plan and billing details. For more information on this error, read the docs: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/error-codes/api-errors.', 'type': 'insufficient_quota', 'param': None, 'code': 'insufficient_quota'}}]
ChatGPT (gpt-4o).ground_truth:
Ans - 0.176...
D = 2.4 m  - this info not required
d = 0.45 mm  - this info is not even required
4*beta = 1.2 cm
refractive index = 1.7
new beta = ((1.2/4)))/1.7 = 0.176... cm

ChatGPT (gpt-4o).subject: Physics
ChatGPT (gpt-4o).question:
Light of frequency 7.21×10^14 Hz is incident on a metal surface.
Electrons are ejected with a maximum speed of 6.0×10^5 m/s.
Calculate the work function of the metal in electron volts (eV).
(Given: h=6.63×10−34 Js, mass of an electron = 9.1×10−31 kg, 1 eV=1.6×10−19 J).
ChatGPT (gpt-4o).response: [ERROR: Error code: 429 - {'error': {'message': 'You exceeded your current quota, please check your plan and billing details. For more information on this error, read the docs: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/error-codes/api-errors.', 'type': 'insufficient_quota', 'param': None, 'code': 'insufficient_quota'}}]
ChatGPT (gpt-4o).ground_truth:
Ans - 1.96 eV

KE = 1/2 mv^2
v = 6*10^5 m/s
total energy = h*freq
freq = 7.21*10^14 Hz
total energy - KE = work function
by plugging in the values, work function in J -
to convert to eV - divide by 1.6 *10^-19

ChatGPT (gpt-4o).subject: Physics
ChatGPT (gpt-4o).question: A parallel plate capacitor is connected to a battery, and then the battery is disconnected. A dielectric slab is then inserted. How does the electric field change?
ChatGPT (gpt-4o).response: [ERROR: Error code: 429 - {'error': {'message': 'You exceeded your current quota, please check your plan and billing details. For more information on this error, read the docs: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/error-codes/api-errors.', 'type': 'insufficient_quota', 'param': None, 'code': 'insufficient_quota'}}]
ChatGPT (gpt-4o).ground_truth:
The trick lies in the sequence: connected (gets charged)
->disconnected (charge is conserved) -> dielectric inserted.
Q remains constant, but V decreases (because
V=V/Kappa), so E decreases (because E=V/d). If the
battery was still connected, V would be constant and Q
would increase.

ChatGPT (gpt-4o).question: Extrapolate on the pretext of the poem "Road Not Taken" by Robert Frost on the basis of the situation of the world at the time it was written and the condition of the poet at that time
ChatGPT (gpt-4o).response: [ERROR: Error code: 429 - {'error': {'message': 'You exceeded your current quota, please check your plan and billing details. For more information on this error, read the docs: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/error-codes/api-errors.', 'type': 'insufficient_quota', 'param': None, 'code': 'insufficient_quota'}}]
ChatGPT (gpt-4o).ground_truth:
This poem was written during the time of the
WW1. The poet, at that time, was at a crucial
time of his life, where he was faced with two
choices that could decide his future. In his case,
he wrote this poem as an ode to a friend who had trouble
making simple decisions. The poem reflects
on their journey on making choices. In the first two stanzas
of the poem, he describes two choices as two paths
at a crossroad. He explains how he tries to look
down as far as possible in both paths referring to
his attempt to forsee his future. However he
explains how this foresight is barred to only a
specific point after which everything is a mystery.
He also describes both paths, one of which is
less trodden upon. This reflects in his life as writing
(his current proffesion) was not a very popular
choice taken up at that time. In the next two stanzas,
he describes his life after he has made his decision
to take the less popular path of writing. He seemed
to know that after he made his decision, it would l
ead onto other ways which would make it impossible
to come back to this one. In the end, he accepts
this and makes his way down his own path. He
ends the poem with a slightly ambiguous note by
saying "I shall be telling this with a sigh".This sigh
may symbolise many emotions such as contentment,
regret, curiousity,etc. This interpretation has been
left to the reader to figure out. To wrap up, the poet
says that the choice was a life - changing one that
made all the difference to him.

Gemini (gemini-2.5-pro).subject: Physics
Gemini (gemini-2.5-pro).question:
In a Young's Double Slit Experiment (YDSE), the slits are separated by 0.45 mm and the screen is placed 2.4 m away.
The distance between the central bright fringe and the fourth bright fringe is measured to be 1.2 cm.
If the entire experimental setup is immersed in a transparent liquid of refractive index μ=1.7, what will be the new fringe width?
Gemini (gemini-2.5-pro).ground_truth:
Ans - 0.176...
D = 2.4 m  - this info not required
d = 0.45 mm  - this info is not even required
4*beta = 1.2 cm
refractive index = 1.7
new beta = ((1.2/4)))/1.7 = 0.176... cm

Gemini (gemini-2.5-pro).subject: Physics
Gemini (gemini-2.5-pro).question:
Light of frequency 7.21×10^14 Hz is incident on a metal surface.
Electrons are ejected with a maximum speed of 6.0×10^5 m/s.
Calculate the work function of the metal in electron volts (eV).
(Given: h=6.63×10−34 Js, mass of an electron = 9.1×10−31 kg, 1 eV=1.6×10−19 J).
Gemini (gemini-2.5-pro).ground_truth:
Ans - 1.96 eV

KE = 1/2 mv^2
v = 6*10^5 m/s
total energy = h*freq
freq = 7.21*10^14 Hz
total energy - KE = work function
by plugging in the values, work function in J -
to convert to eV - divide by 1.6 *10^-19

Gemini (gemini-2.5-pro).subject: Physics
Gemini (gemini-2.5-pro).question: A parallel plate capacitor is connected to a battery, and then the battery is disconnected. A dielectric slab is then inserted. How does the electric field change?
Gemini (gemini-2.5-pro).ground_truth:
The trick lies in the sequence: connected (gets charged)
->disconnected (charge is conserved) -> dielectric inserted.
Q remains constant, but V decreases (because
V=V/Kappa), so E decreases (because E=V/d). If the
battery was still connected, V would be constant and Q
would increase.

Gemini (gemini-2.5-pro).subject: English
Gemini (gemini-2.5-pro).question: Extrapolate on the pretext of the poem "Road Not Taken" by Robert Frost on the basis of the situation of the world at the time it was written and the condition of the poet at that time
Gemini (gemini-2.5-pro).ground_truth:
This poem was written during the time of the
WW1. The poet, at that time, was at a crucial
time of his life, where he was faced with two
choices that could decide his future. In his case,
he wrote this poem as an ode to a friend who had trouble
making simple decisions. The poem reflects
on their journey on making choices. In the first two stanzas
of the poem, he describes two choices as two paths
at a crossroad. He explains how he tries to look
down as far as possible in both paths referring to
his attempt to forsee his future. However he
explains how this foresight is barred to only a
specific point after which everything is a mystery.
He also describes both paths, one of which is
less trodden upon. This reflects in his life as writing
(his current proffesion) was not a very popular
choice taken up at that time. In the next two stanzas,
he describes his life after he has made his decision
to take the less popular path of writing. He seemed
to know that after he made his decision, it would l
ead onto other ways which would make it impossible
to come back to this one. In the end, he accepts
this and makes his way down his own path. He
ends the poem with a slightly ambiguous note by
saying "I shall be telling this with a sigh".This sigh
may symbolise many emotions such as contentment,
regret, curiousity,etc. This interpretation has been
left to the reader to figure out. To wrap up, the poet
says that the choice was a life - changing one that
made all the difference to him.
Gemini (gemini-2.5-pro).reasoning: Expected number 1.0 not found in response.
